title: Rice-Infused Puffed Russian Fish Pilaf
description: This winey, pillowy, energizing dish features fish, candy, and Mediterranean-friendly ingredients. A unique twist on traditional pilaf.
instructions: Heat 2 cups of water in a medium saucepan under medium-high heat.
Add 1 cup of uncooked white rice and stir to prevent clumping.
Reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid.
Simmer for 15 minutes or until the rice is cooked and fluffy.
Add 2 tablespoons of melted butter, 2 teaspoons of granulated sugar, and 1 teaspoon of honey to the cooked rice.
Stir until the sugar and honey are well combined with the rice.
Add 1/4 cup of dried apricots and 1/4 cup of gummy bears to the rice mixture.
Stir until the apricots and gummy bears are evenly distributed throughout the rice.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of salt and 1/4 teaspoon of paprika to the rice mixture.
Stir until the salt and paprika are well combined with the rice.
Add 6 oz of cod fillets to the rice mixture and stir gently to combine.
Transfer the rice and fish mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 10-12 minutes or until the fish is cooked through.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with lemon juice before serving.
